summary: Iteration 6 end-to-end verified. Backend: all 3 tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6.py pass (interestedIn saves on PUT /api/users/me + visible on self GET, stripped on GET /api/users/{uid} when privacy.interestedIn=true, visible when not private). Full backend suite: 83 passed, 3 skipped, 5 pre-existing failures unchanged (TestDiscoveryAndLikes seed-dependent + test_post_with_mp4_via_media — carry-over from iter-3/4/5, explicitly out of scope). Discovery router confirmed to pipe interestedIn through user_to_profile + strip_private_fields → DiscoveryProfile payload. Frontend: Playwright signup, opened /profile, clicked Edit Profile, verified data-testid=profile-interested-in-input + data-testid=profile-interested-in-private both render, filled 'tournament partners, doubles, casual rounds', clicked Save — page returned to view mode and 'How others see you' card rendered data-testid=public-profile-interested-in with literal text 'Interested in: tournament partners, doubles, casual rounds'. Discovery grid loaded with 24 cards; each card is wrapped in PublicProfilePreview (data-testid=public-profile-preview, banner, thumbnail). One card showed 'Interested in: doubles night every thursday' confirming the field flows through /api/discovery. Action row present per card (nice-btn-{uid}, message-btn-{uid}, player-btn-{uid}). Clicking Nice fired toast 'Nice — you tagged them 💚' and URL stayed on /discovery (stopPropagation works). Responsive grid classes confirmed: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3. Banner click on a card navigates to /players/{uid} as expected. Existing regressions (radius bar 'Within: 10/25/50/100/250 mi', invite banner, location hint) still render correctly.

issues: When player.name is empty/null, the h2 still renders an empty <h2> above the skillLevel line, leaving a small extra gap. Mostly invisible but consider conditionally rendering the h2 when both name and age are absent (cosmetic only, not blocking).

action_items: (Carry-over from iter 3/4/5) 5 seed-dependent tests in test_api.py + test_new_features.py still fail in the full suite. Not a regression; explicitly out of scope per iteration-6 note. Re-enable seed_demo_users behind a DEV flag or rewrite those tests to seed their own fixtures.
critical_code_review_comments: PublicProfilePreview.jsx — the 'actions' slot is rendered inside the same overlap container; Discovery wraps each card in a div with role=button + onClick=openProfile and onKeyDown=Enter/Space. Action buttons use both onClick=stopPropagation on the wrapping div AND e.stopPropagation()/e.preventDefault() inside each handler — defensive and correct. No double-fire observed.

Discovery.jsx — handleNice/handleMessage/handlePlayer all call both e.stopPropagation() and e.preventDefault(). The actions wrapper div ALSO has onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}. This is belt-and-suspenders but harmless. Could simplify by relying on the wrapper div alone.

PublicProfilePreview.jsx — when player.name is null/empty, the

still renders (with just an empty string and no comma). Minor cosmetic; consider `{(player.name || player.age) &&

...

}` to drop the empty heading on bare profiles.
deps.PRIVATE_FIELDS — clean addition of 'interestedIn'. Same shape as the other 4 private fields. No other code paths needed updating because strip_private_fields iterates the tuple.

models.ProfileIn.interestedIn — Pydantic max_length=200 matches the user request. ProfileOut.interestedIn correctly Optional[str] and defaulted to None. Discovery propagation via DiscoveryProfile(ProfileOut) inherits the field for free.
Discovery card body click also fires on keyboard Enter/Space — accessibility is good. The wrapping div has aria-label=`Open ${player.name}'s full profile`. Nice touch.

success_rate.backend: 100% on iteration-6 features (3/3 in test_iteration6.py). Full suite: 83 passed, 3 skipped, 5 pre-existing failures unchanged from prior iterations.
success_rate.frontend: 100% on iteration-6 features. Profile edit (interested-in field + privacy toggle + Save persistence + PublicProfilePreview render), Discovery PublicProfilePreview-based grid, action-button stopPropagation, responsive grid, card-body navigation all verified.

test_credentials: Signed up TEST_i6prof_<random>@example.com and TEST_i6ui_<random>@example.com via the UI signup form (Firebase Identity Toolkit). Demo password demo1234. Backend tests use TEST_i6a_*/TEST_i6b_* and clean up via accounts:delete.
seed_data_creation: None. Each Playwright run creates its own ephemeral user and an interestedIn value through the API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 backend tests: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6.py (3 tests). Run only iter-6: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iteration6.py -v`. Full regression: `python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v` — expect 83 passed + 5 pre-existing failures (TestDiscoveryAndLikes seed-dependent + test_post_with_mp4_via_media). New selectors: data-testid=profile-interested-in-input, profile-interested-in-private, public-profile-interested-in. Discovery card structure: each card wraps PublicProfilePreview — selectors data-testid=discovery-card-{uid} (outer), public-profile-preview, public-profile-banner, public-profile-thumbnail (inner), and action row nice-btn-{uid}/message-btn-{uid}/player-btn-{uid} (or player-status-sent-{uid}/player-status-friends-{uid}). Card body click → /players/{uid}; action buttons stopPropagation. To reach the edit form on Profile.jsx you must click 'Edit Profile' (button:has-text('Edit Profile') or [data-testid=profile-edit-btn] if added).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All iteration-6 features land exactly per spec: backend privacy strip extended to 'interestedIn', Discovery payload propagates the field, Profile edit form gained the labeled textarea + privacy toggle, PublicProfilePreview renders the new line below 'Favorite Frisbee', Discovery grid swapped its card template for PublicProfilePreview while keeping the existing action handlers wired through the optional `actions` slot. stopPropagation behavior verified — Nice click does not navigate; banner click does navigate to /players/{uid}.
